title: US policy, gangs and climate change are reshaping Central America
content: Migration and democratic decline in Central America cannot be understood separately from the intertwined impacts of US intervention, gang violence, economic instability and climate disruption. As droughts, displacement and insecurity deepen, the region faces growing pressure toward both migration and authoritarian rule.

creator: Rebecca Gordon

title: Rebuilding after wildfire: Paradise, California hosts a gathering on community resilience
content: A gathering in Paradise, California, will bring together fire-affected communities, local leaders and resilience practitioners to explore what rebuilding after catastrophe can look like beyond simply restoring the old normal.

creator: Bob Stilger

title: Wars destroy lives and the climate. Why aren’t we counting military emissions?
content: War is a major driver of greenhouse gas emissions, yet most conflict-related emissions remain excluded from official climate accounting. Governments and international climate bodies must begin treating military emissions and the climate costs of war as central issues of accountability and justice.

creator: Tamara Krawchenko

title: In the Rising Tide, Episode 5. Raviraj Shetty: Rewriting the Stories We Live By
content: What if the stories we live by could change? In India, Raviraj Shetty explores how narrative, care, and imagination can help communities heal, reclaim dignity, and find new ways forward.
snippet: What if the stories we live by could change? In India, Raviraj Shetty explores how narrative, care, and imagination can help communities heal, reclaim dignity, and find new ways forward.
creator: Alex Leff

title: Iran war analysis: How 60 nations have responded to the global energy crisis
content: One month into the US and Israel’s war on Iran, at least 60 countries have taken emergency measures in response to the subsequent global energy crisis, according to analysis by Carbon Brief.
snippet: One month into the US and Israel’s war on Iran, at least 60 countries have taken emergency measures in response to the subsequent global energy crisis, according to analysis by Carbon Brief.
creator: Josh Gabbatiss
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Key outcomes from the first summit on ‘transitioning away’ from fossil fuels
content: Countries attending a first-of-its-kind summit have walked away with plans to develop national roadmaps away from fossil fuels, along with new tools to address harmful subsidies and carbon-intensive trade.

creator: Daisy Dunne

title: How environmental destruction is built into corporate design
content: Modern corporations are legally and financially structured to prioritize profit over ecological stability. The result is a system that normalizes environmental destruction while diffusing responsibility across institutions and individuals.

creator: Saskia Karges

title: Heat‑resistant corals could help reefs adapt to climate change
content: Efforts to save coral reefs from climate‑driven heatwaves focus on conserving and breeding heat‑resistant corals and harnessing resilient algae and bacteria, offering a test case for conservation in a warming world.

title: A perspective from Lebanon: Who will we be when things get hard?
content: Nate Hagens steps away from analysis and reflects on a call that reframed his thinking. He shares a recent conversation with a close friend living in Lebanon, who, amid ongoing daily violence and loss, has been hosting displaced families and leading meditation practices in her community.

title: Environmental protection depends on more than regulation
content: Wetlands, rivers and ecosystems do not stop functioning when legal definitions change. The long-term stability of environmental protection may depend less on enforcement than on cultural attitudes toward nature itself.

creator: Sepala Weliwitigoda

title: We have entered the age of consequences for climate and energy inaction: An interview with Richard Heinberg
content: In the final part of this three-part interview series, Richard Heinberg reflects on decades of ignored warnings about energy descent and economic collapse, calling for a voluntary relocalization of economics and politics.
